2604.00886.title: PixelPrune: Pixel-Level Adaptive Visual Token Reduction via Predictive Coding
2604.00886.paper_summary: Document understanding and GUI interaction are among the highest-value applications of Vision-Language Models (VLMs), yet they impose exceptionally heavy computational burden: fine-grained text and small UI elements demand high-resolution inputs that produce tens of thousands of visual tokens. We observe that this cost is largely wasteful -- across document and GUI benchmarks, only 22--71\% of image patches are pixel-unique, the rest being exact duplicates of another patch in the same image. We propose \textbf{PixelPrune}, which exploits this pixel-level redundancy through predictive-coding-based compression, pruning redundant patches \emph{before} the Vision Transformer (ViT) encoder. Because it operates in pixel space prior to any neural computation, PixelPrune accelerates both the ViT encoder and the downstream LLM, covering the full inference pipeline. The method is training-free, requires no learnable parameters, and supports pixel-lossless compression ($τ{=}0$) as well as controlled lossy compression ($τ{>}0$). Experiments across three model scales and document and GUI benchmarks show that PixelPrune maintains competitive task accuracy while delivering up to 4.2$\times$ inference speedup and 1.9$\times$ training acceleration. Code is available at https://github.com/OPPO-Mente-Lab/PixelPrune.
2604.00886.paper_summary_zh: 文件理解和圖形用戶界面互動是視覺-語言模型（VLMs）中最具價值的應用之一，但它們卻帶來了極大的計算負擔：細粒度的文本和小型用戶界面元素需要高解析度的輸入，這會產生數萬個視覺標記。我們觀察到這種成本在很大程度上是浪費的——在文件和圖形用戶界面基準測試中，只有22%到71%的圖像區塊是像素唯一的，其餘則是同一圖像中另一個區塊的精確副本。我們提出了\textbf{PixelPrune}，它通過基於預測編碼的壓縮利用這種像素級冗餘，在視覺Transformer（ViT）編碼器之前修剪冗餘的區塊。由於它在任何神經計算之前在像素空間中運行，PixelPrune加速了ViT編碼器和下游的LLM，涵蓋了完整的推斷管道。該方法無需訓練，無需可學習參數，並支持像素無損壓縮（$τ{=}0$）以及可控的有損壓縮（$τ{>}0$）。在三種模型規模以及文件和圖形用戶界面基準測試中的實驗顯示，PixelPrune在保持競爭性任務準確性的同時，提供了高達4.2$\times$的推斷加速和1.9$\times$的訓練加速。代碼可在https://github.com/OPPO-Mente-Lab/PixelPrune獲得。
2604.00886.author: Nan Wang et.al.
2604.00886.authors: Nan Wang, Zhiwei Jin, Chen Chen, Haonan Lu
